pr.title: PR #153 — CLI Validation Evidence Recorder v1
pr.description:
## Summary Adds an Axiom-native way to run an **explicit validation plan** (an ordered list of allowlisted CLI commands) and write a **durable evidence bundle** — so validation proof is repo/artifact-addressable instead of living in manual terminal copy/paste, Devin session context, or screenshots. Validation-evidence infrastructure only: it **reuses** existing command-registry governance and `is_within_sandbox` path safety; it is **not** a new runner, retry loop, implementation worker, or promotion change. `EvidenceRunner` (PR #25) only covers three fixed in-process validations; this recorder is complementary — it runs an arbitrary plan of safe CLI commands as subprocesses and captures per-command evidence. ## Scope - New module `src/axiom_core/validation/cli_validation_recorder.py`: plan format, governance gate, executor, and bundle writer. - New CLI command `axiom cli-validation-record` in `src/axiom_cli/main.py` + a registry entry in `command_registry.py`. - Example plans + docs under `docs/validation_plans/` (M4 execution-chain, M2 evidence-promotion). - Tests, behavior-change ledger entry (BHV-029), and a small skill-checklist reference. ## Key Changes CLI surface: ``` axiom cli-validation-record --plan \ [--artifacts-root

] [--name ] [--set KEY=VALUE] [--dry-run] [--json-output] ``` Governance — every plan command is authorized against the Runner Command Registry; only cataloged, `SafetyLevel.SAFE`, non-Revit commands run (no shell, explicit `argv`): ```python def authorize_command(name) -> GovernanceDecision: spec = command_registry.get_command(name) if spec is None: return denied("not in registry") if spec.requires_revit: return denied("requires live Revit") if spec.safety_level is not SAFE: return denied(f"classified '{...}'") return allowed() ``` Run policy — failures are first-class. A failed/blocked command (assertion fail, unexpected exit, timeout) with `continue_on_failure=false` stops the run and marks the rest `skipped`; run status is `passed` only if every command passed. Process exit code is `0` pass / `1` otherwise. Path safety — bundle dir and `expect_artifact_exists` checks route through `artifact_paths.is_within_sandbox` (POSIX + Windows safe; traversal rejected), reusing the PR #151 fix rather than re-implementing containment. Bundle layout (under git-ignored `artifacts/validation_evidence//`): ``` validation_run.json commands.json environment.json artifact_manifest.json (sha256/file) assertion_results.json plan_snapshot.json report.md commands/NN_.stdout.txt commands/NN_.stderr.txt ``` Plan format is a small explicit JSON list (deliberately **not** a workflow DSL): `plan_id`, `title`, `purpose`, and `commands[]` with `id`, `command`, `args` (supporting `${artifacts_root}` / `${run_dir}` / `${repo_root}` / `--set` substitution), `timeout_seconds`, `expected_exit_code`, `expect_stdout_contains`, `expect_stderr_contains`, `expect_artifact_exists`, `continue_on_failure`. The default `SubprocessCommandExecutor` runs `poetry run axiom `; an injectable `CommandExecutor` protocol keeps tests subprocess-free. ## Validation Ubuntu (Devin): - `tests/test_cli_validation_recorder.py` — 26 tests (plan parsing, governance, success/failure/timeout/blocked/skip, variable substitution, dry-run, path-safety) — pass. - Directive-required targeted tests: `test_execution_chain_orchestrator.py`, `test_evidence_promotion.py`, `test_local_runner.py`, `test_command_registry.py` (+ recorder) — **141 passed / 1 skipped**. - Full suite — **5098 passed / 1 skipped**; `poetry run ruff check .` — clean. - CLI smoke test produced real bundles: M4 plan → `Status: PASSED 1/1`; M2 plan (with `--set evidence=`) → `Status: PASSED 2/2`. `git status` confirms generated bundles stay under git-ignored `artifacts/validation_evidence/`. Pending operator (Windows) — cannot run in Devin's Ubuntu env: re-run M4 + M2 plans via `axiom cli-validation-record` on Windows to confirm subprocess/path behavior on the real target. ## Non-Goals No new runner framework. No retry loop. No implementation-worker behavior. No Local Runner rewrite. No GPR/global PR registry. No canonical doctrine. No confidence-math / evidence-promotion-semantic / execution-chain ID-flow changes. No Revit-live validation. No browser/terminal recording or Devin session ingestion. No broad refactor. ## Architectural Significance Strengthens the verification factory's **evidence quality**: validation proof becomes durable, structured, and addressable, with governance and path-safety reused from existing systems rather than duplicated. Complementary to `EvidenceRunner`, not a replacement. --- ### Purpose-to-Workflow Reconciliation 1. **Existing PR/component/capability used:** Runner Command Registry (PR #22) for governance; `artifact_paths.is_within_sandbox` (PR #151) for path safety; the Local Runner subprocess/env-capture pattern; `execution-chain-run` (PR #146) and `capability-evidence-apply` (PR #147/#148) as the commands the example plans drive. 2. **Workflow it participates in:** the validation/evidence lane of the verification factory — runs governed CLI sequences and persists proof. 3. **What consumes its output:** humans/operators reviewing `report.md`; downstream tooling reading `validation_run.json` / `commands.json` / `artifact_manifest.json`; the Windows Local Runner validation lane. 4. **Evidence it works:** 26 recorder tests + 141 targeted + 5098 full-suite pass; ruff clean; real M4/M2 bundles produced via CLI smoke test. 5. **Disposition:** validation artifact / support infrastructure (compatibility-friendly), not a new engine. 6. **Existing structure checked before adding:** `EvidenceRunner` (3 fixed in-process validations — cannot run an arbitrary plan), Local Runner (named allowlisted actions only), command registry (governance, executes nothing). New code is the minimal plan-driven recorder layer over these. 7. **Risks introduced:** subprocess latency (bounded by per-command timeout); a thin new module — mitigated by reuse of governance + path safety and an injectable executor. No duplicate-engine: complementary to `EvidenceRunner`. 8. **Delete/merge/preserve/leave alone:** preserve `EvidenceRunner` and Local Runner; leave confidence/promotion/execution-chain semantics alone; add only the recorder + plans + tests. ### Pre-Review Self-Audit 1. Plan format is small/explicit, not a workflow DSL — yes. 2. Every command authorized via the command registry before execution — yes (`authorize_command`). 3. Only `SAFE`, non-Revit, cataloged commands run by default; everything else `blocked` — yes. 4. No arbitrary shell strings; explicit `argv`, no `shell=True` — yes. 5. Per-command id/args/cwd/timing/exit/timed-out/stdout/stderr/env captured — yes. 6. Durable bundle with both machine-readable + human-readable outputs — yes. 7. M4 + M2 example plans provided and parse/run — yes. 8. Failures first-class (recorded, outputs preserved, run failed, later commands skipped per policy) — yes. 9. No retry implemented — yes. 10. Path safety reuses `is_within_sandbox`; no hard-coded `/` or `\`; traversal rejected — yes. 11. Windows + POSIX path handling preserved (pathlib + reused helper) — yes. 12. Generated bundles kept out of git (`artifacts/validation_evidence/` ignored) — yes. 13. No confidence-math / evidence-promotion-semantic / execution-chain ID-flow changes — yes. 14. No new canonical doctrine, no GPR, no Devin/screenshot/video ingestion — yes. 15. SKILL.md touched only with a small justified reference (new command checklist) — yes. Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7 Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev
